legal_drinking_age.description: Minimum age to purchase, possess, or consume alcohol in Alberta

age_verification_required.description: Age verification must be performed at point of sale and at point of delivery

age_verification_id_check_threshold.description: Licensees must request ID from anyone who appears under this age

proserve_certification_required.description: All staff working under a liquor licence authority, including delivery drivers, must hold valid ProServe Liquor Staff Training certification

proserve_validity_years.description: ProServe certification expires after this many years from the completion date

delivery_sealed_container.description: All liquor delivered must be in a sealed, tamper-proof commercial container with contents clearly identified on the label
delivery_sealed_container.handbook_ref: Liquor Licensee Handbook s.3.1, 3.2; Liquor Manufacturer Handbook s.3.7, 3.8

delivery_within_alberta.description: Liquor delivery licensees must deliver within Alberta

delivery_common_carrier_permitted.description: Licensees may use a common carrier for liquor delivery

off_sales_hours.description: Maximum hours for off-premises liquor sales (takeout/delivery)

drive_through_sales_prohibited.description: Off sales from a drive-through window are not allowed

class_a_off_premises_delivery.description: Class A licensees are permitted to sell liquor for off-site consumption via takeout or delivery, with or without food purchase

class_a_mixed_drinks_delivery.description: Class A licensees may sell mixed drinks from their in-house menu for delivery and off-premises consumption
class_a_mixed_drinks_delivery.source: AGLC Class A Off Sales bulletin, Modernization amendments

markup_rates_effective_date.description: Date the current AGLC markup rate schedule took effect

markup_spirits_gt60_abv.description: Standard markup rate for spirits greater than 60% ABV

markup_spirits_gt22_lte60_abv.description: Standard markup rate for spirits greater than 22% and up to 60% ABV

markup_spirits_lte22_abv.description: Standard markup rate for spirits up to 22% ABV

markup_refreshment_gt8_lte16_abv.description: Standard markup rate for refreshment beverages greater than 8% and up to 16% ABV

markup_refreshment_gt1_lte8_abv.description: Standard markup rate for refreshment beverages greater than 1% and up to 8% ABV

markup_wine_gt16_abv.description: Standard markup rate for wine (incl. fruit wine, mead, sake) greater than 16% ABV

markup_wine_lte16_abv.description: Standard markup rate for wine (incl. fruit wine, mead, sake) up to 16% ABV

markup_beer_gt22_lte60_abv.description: Standard markup rate for beer greater than 22% and up to 60% ABV

markup_beer_gt16_lte22_abv.description: Standard markup rate for beer greater than 16% and up to 22% ABV

markup_beer_gt119_lte16_abv.description: Standard markup rate for beer greater than 11.9% and up to 16% ABV

markup_beer_lte119_abv.description: Standard markup rate for beer up to 11.9% ABV

markup_wine_ad_valorem_tier1.description: Additional ad valorem markup on wine with invoice price greater than $15/L and up to $20/L
markup_wine_ad_valorem_tier1.source: AGLC Liquor Markup Rates and Services bulletin (2025-02-28)

markup_wine_ad_valorem_tier2.description: Additional ad valorem markup on wine with invoice price greater than $20/L and up to $25/L

markup_wine_ad_valorem_tier3.description: Additional ad valorem markup on wine with invoice price greater than $25/L

non_alcoholic_exempt.description: Products containing 0.5% ABV or less are not subject to AGLC markup or excise duty

all_products_must_be_registered.description: All liquor products sold in Alberta must be registered with AGLC

canadian_labelling_required.description: All liquor products sold in Alberta must meet Canadian labelling requirements for alcoholic beverages

aglc_is_importer_of_record.description: All liquor products in Alberta must be sold through AGLC. AGLC is the legal purchaser (importer of record); the liquor agency is the seller

agency_registration_required.description: A supplier must either appoint a registered agency or register its own company as a liquor agency to represent its products in Alberta

agency_registration_term_years.description: A liquor agency registration has a fixed term, after which it must be renewed

agency_registration_fee.description: Fee for liquor agency registration or renewal

agency_inactive_termination_months.description: Registration may be terminated if the agency has not shipped a liquor product to the Alberta marketplace for this many months

product_registration_processing_weeks.description: AGLC product registration takes up to this many weeks to process. Issues identified during review must be resolved before completion and may further delay.

product_code_six_digit.description: Each registered product receives a unique 6-digit AGLC product code for ordering and inventory tracking

container_registration_bcmb_required.description: All beverage containers must be registered with the Beverage Container Management Board (BCMB) before LAP product registration. Exempt containers (kegs, wooden casks/barrels) still require information provided to AGLC

price_change_frequency.description: Product price changes are permitted on a weekly basis. Changes submitted by 4:00 PM Wednesday take effect Friday of the following week

uniform_wholesale_pricing.description: All licensees pay the same wholesale price for a given liquor product, regardless of quantity purchased or licensee type

wholesale_price_composition.description: The wholesale price paid by licensees is composed of the invoice price plus recycling fee, excise duty (if applicable), provincial markup, GST, and container deposit

consignment_basis.description: All liquor advanced shipping notices to the AGLC warehouse are on a consignment basis only

product_cannot_ship_out_of_alberta.description: Product held at the AGLC warehouse cannot be shipped out of Alberta

aglc_retains_sample_analysis_right.description: AGLC retains the right to request a sample of any product for analysis. Products found to be unsafe may be discontinued

prohibited_inducements.description: Liquor suppliers and agencies are prohibited from offering or giving licensees inducements to favour their products. Licensees are prohibited from accepting or requesting such inducements. Penalties range from licence cancellation to a fine up to the entire value of the inducement
prohibited_inducements.statute_ref: Gaming, Liquor and Cannabis Regulation s.81(e), s.82(2)

advertising_must_not_encourage_irrespons.description: All marketing and promotion of liquor products sold in Alberta must not encourage the irresponsible use, consumption, or service of liquor. Advertising must encourage legal, moderate, and safe consumption

advertising_no_minors.description: No minor, or anyone who may reasonably be mistaken for a minor, may appear in advertising for a liquor product

advertising_no_minor_appeal_personalitie.description: No well-known personality or look-alike with strong appeal to minors may be featured in liquor advertising

advertising_no_vehicle_operation.description: Advertising must not show any scene of a person with liquor before or while operating a vehicle (motorized or not) or doing any activity considered dangerous

advertising_comparative_allowed.description: Comparative price advertising is allowed but must not disparage a competitor or competitor's product

delivery_service_advertising_restriction.description: Class D liquor delivery service advertising must not promote the use, sale, or consumption of liquor

delivery_not_to_intoxicated_person.description: Liquor must not be delivered to an intoxicated person

delivery_order_leave_during_business_hou.description: A liquor delivery order must leave the licensed premises during regular business hours; however, delivery may continue for 30 minutes after closing

delivery_service_storage_on_premises.description: Delivery service licensees are permitted to store liquor on their licensed premises or another location specified on their licence. They must still purchase liquor from a retail liquor store, general merchandise store, or off-sales licensee
delivery_service_storage_on_premises.source: AGLC Bulletin: Delivery Service Licence Storage (Dec 2025)

delivery_purchase_source.description: Delivery service licensees must purchase their liquor from a retail liquor store, general merchandise store, or an off-sales licensee

no_third_party_delivery_platforms_restri.description: AGLC rules require delivery to be conducted by the licensee's staff, contracted drivers, or common carriers. There is no explicit prohibition on third-party delivery platforms for liquor (unlike cannabis), but all delivery personnel must hold ProServe certification and verify age at delivery
no_third_party_delivery_platforms_restri.l2laaf_note: L2LAAF marketplace drivers delivering alcohol must hold ProServe and perform age verification regardless of employment relationship

class_e_manufacturer_agency_automatic.description: A liquor supplier licensed as a Class E manufacturer in Alberta is automatically registered as a liquor agency and does not need a separate agency application

letter_of_authorization_required.description: When a supplier engages a registered agency to represent its products, the supplier must complete a Letter of Authorization designating the agency, and the agency must complete a Letter of Understanding confirming the agreement

default_deny_principle.description: An activity not specifically permitted under the AGLC handbook guidelines is prohibited
default_deny_principle.l2laaf_note: Any marketplace feature involving liquor must be explicitly permitted by AGLC policy. If unclear, assume prohibited and flag for legal review.

proserve_mandatory_delivery_drivers.description: ProServe certification is mandatory for delivery service drivers under all licence classes (A, B, D, E, F and Duty Free)

proserve_new_hire_grace_period_days.description: New hires to positions requiring ProServe must be certified within this many days of their employment start date

proserve_proof_forms.description: Acceptable proof of ProServe certification: printed certificate with QR code, or a clear digital image (screenshot) saved on the staff member's mobile device

proserve_conditional_status_violations.description: ProServe certificate placed into conditional status when worker violates: supplying liquor to a minor (GLCA s.75), supplying to intoxicated person (GLCA s.75.1), or failure to check ID for persons appearing under 25

proserve_conditional_recertify_days.description: Worker with conditional ProServe status must retake the program and pay fees within this many days of AGLC notification, or certificate is cancelled

proserve_permanent_ban_violation_count.description: A worker who incurs this many violations within a 5-year period is permanently ineligible for ProServe certification
proserve_permanent_ban_violation_count.l2laaf_note: L2LAAF must track violation count per driver and permanently bar drivers who reach 3 violations in 5 years from alcohol delivery assignments

proserve_equivalency_other_provinces.description: ProServe equivalency may be granted for certificates issued by other provinces if acquired within the last 5 years. Worker must apply to info@smartprograms.aglc.ca

ownership_change_reporting_threshold.description: A sale, assignment or transfer of 5% or more of a liquor business must be reported to and approved by AGLC prior to the effective date

registration_not_transferable.description: An agency registration is not transferable

competitive_relationship_required.description: The relationship between a supplier or agency and a licensee must be competitive in nature

licensee_cannot_own_agency.description: A licensee, a licensee's associates, or employee of the licensee is not permitted to own, operate or manage a supplier or agency

licensee_cannot_advise_or_finance_agency.description: A licensee, licensee's associates or employee is not permitted to act as an advisor or provide financial support to a supplier or agency

no_exclusive_product_via_allocation.description: A supplier or agency cannot be set up to provide an exclusive product to a licensee or group of licensees through the allocation process or any other means
no_exclusive_product_via_allocation.l2laaf_note: PROOF marketplace must not facilitate exclusive product arrangements between agencies and specific licensees

one_agency_per_product.description: Each product must not be represented by more than one agency at the same time

agency_prohibited_selling_outside_aglc.description: A liquor agency is prohibited from selling liquor other than through AGLC
agency_prohibited_selling_outside_aglc.l2laaf_note: All PROOF marketplace transactions for agency-represented products must flow through the AGLC wholesale system

no_deposits_except_futures.description: An agency may not accept deposits for liquor aside from futures (products purchased min. 12 months prior to bottling)

sub_agents_permitted.description: An agency may contract or employ the services of a different registered agency (sub-agent) to represent its products

no_minimum_order_quantity.description: Agencies are not permitted to impose a minimum order quantity per product per licensee
no_minimum_order_quantity.l2laaf_note: PROOF marketplace must not enforce minimum order quantities on behalf of agencies

maximum_order_quantity_permitted.description: Agencies may impose a maximum order quantity per product per licensee

product_allocation_limit.description: Allocation of a product is limited to 1,000 cases per product code per calendar year. Not allowed if product had >2,000 case sales in the previous year or attains 2,000 in the current year. Private label products may exceed this limit.

wine_standard_container_sizes.description: Wine may only be offered for sale to licensees in standard container sizes. Non-standard sizes only for food service, special event licensees, or promotional purposes.
wine_standard_container_sizes.statute_ref: Federal Consumer Packaging and Labelling Regulation s.36(1)(c)

abandoned_product_criteria.description: Product is considered abandoned in the AGLC Central Warehouse if: agency registration lapses >90 days, agency has not responded to AGLC communication within 90 days, or no sales for 24 consecutive months. Abandoned product may be destroyed.

product_auto_archive_months.description: Products with no activity for 12 months are automatically updated to Archive status by AGLC

consignment_payment_frequency.description: Payments for consignment orders are made weekly for all product sold the previous week

product_description_accuracy.description: Product description must accurately reflect the actual product: consistency between registered name and label name, consistent names across size configurations, and organic products must include 'organic' in the name

one_product_code_per_registration.description: Each product must have its own unique product registration. One AGLC product code cannot be used for different, multiple product registrations or sales entries

private_label_rules.description: Private label products (bearing a licensee's name or trademark) must be approved by AGLC, priced at or above the lowest general listed product in the same category, and may only be sold by the licensee whose name appears on the label. Cannot be sold between Class D licensees. Agencies cannot accept deposits for private labels.
private_label_rules.l2laaf_note: PROOF marketplace must enforce private label product visibility restrictions — only the named licensee can purchase

advertising_must_not_encourage_non_drink.description: Advertising must not encourage non-drinkers to consume liquor

advertising_no_heavy_consumption.description: Advertising must not show heavy or prolonged liquor consumption

advertising_no_health_benefit_claims.description: Advertising must not give the impression that liquor benefits a person's health

advertising_no_childrens_content.description: The use or imitation of children's fairy tales, jingles, nursery rhymes, songs, musical themes, or fictional characters from children's books is prohibited in liquor advertising

advertising_max_one_drink_per_person.description: In drinking scenes depicted in brand advertising, the quantity of liquor shown in a social setting must not exceed one drink per person

agency_cannot_pay_licensee_advertising.description: An agency is not allowed to pay any advertising costs for a licensee, either directly or indirectly
agency_cannot_pay_licensee_advertising.l2laaf_note: PROOF and Kaskflow must not facilitate agency-funded advertising that appears to come from a licensee

cooperative_advertising_licensee_pays.description: Co-operative advertising (mentioning specific agencies/suppliers) is permitted only if the licensee pays all costs and keeps records for 2 years

prohibited_inducements_no_loyalty_progra.description: An agency is prohibited from participating in any way in a licensee's customer loyalty program, and a licensee may not request agency participation
prohibited_inducements_no_loyalty_progra.l2laaf_note: If PROOF or Kaskflow implements a loyalty program, agency/supplier participation must be blocked

prohibited_inducements_no_recreational_a.description: An agency is prohibited from paying for a licensee's activities that are recreational or leisurely in nature

prohibited_inducements_specific_items.description: An agency may not offer or provide to a licensee: cash/rebates/coupons/credits, deposits into licensee accounts, free liquor (except for sampling per s.5.6), or compensation for interior decorating, renovations, furniture, kitchen/bar equipment, fixtures, refrigeration/dispensing equipment (with exceptions), or building infrastructure/utilities

permitted_non_essential_items.description: Items an agency MAY provide to licensees: aprons/hats, bar towels, bottle openers, branded interior signs (not showing licensee name), clocks, coasters, condiment caddies, draught tap handles, drip mats, flags/pennants/banners, fruit slicers, glasses/mugs, ice buckets, inflatables, lapel pins, menu printing, mirrors, napkins, patio umbrellas, place mats, portable patio heaters, posters, promotional fridges (per s.5.2.10a), promotional racks (per s.5.2.10b), sandwich boards, sanitation stations, serving trays, tent cards/inserts

promotional_agreements_record_retention.description: Records of promotional agreements must be kept by both agency and licensee for a minimum of 2 years and provided to AGLC on request

promotional_items_no_cash.description: Cash, cheques, gift cards, or prepaid credit cards are prohibited as promotional items

promotional_agreements_no_exclusion.description: Promotional agreements cannot result in excluding or prohibiting any competitor's product(s)
promotional_agreements_no_exclusion.l2laaf_note: PROOF marketplace promotional features must not allow exclusion of competitor products

tastings_agencies_cannot_charge.description: Agencies are not licensed to sell liquor and may not charge a fee to customers for liquor tastings, including tastings conducted by a Class D licensee on behalf of the agency

tastings_not_outside_premises.description: Tastings by agencies must not be conducted outside of the licensed premises (e.g. on a patio outside the store, in a tent outside the store)

tasting_staffing_max_hourly_rate.description: When a Class D licensee conducts tastings on behalf of an agency, staffing costs charged to the agency are capped at this hourly rate (wages, benefits, and admin costs)

added_value_max_percentage.description: The cost of an added value (on-pack) promotional item must not exceed 20% of the wholesale price of the liquor product being promoted

warehouse_central_cls_scope.description: The Central AGLC Authorized Warehouse (CLS, St. Albert) stores and distributes spirits, wine, coolers, and beer. Other Authorized Warehouses distribute domestic beer and specified refreshment beverages only.

warehouse_charges_auto_deducted.description: All warehousing charges are automatically deducted from the consignment payee's account. If consignment payments do not equal or exceed warehouse charges, the agency must pay the outstanding difference to AGLC.

warehouse_non_registered_surcharges.description: Non-Registered Advanced Shipping Notice Surcharge and Non-Registered Product Listing Surcharge are incurred when an agency ships product without following proper registration and ASN procedures

warehouse_unpaid_charges_abandonment.description: If an agency does not pay outstanding warehouse charges within the specified notification period, the agency's remaining product will be deemed abandoned by AGLC and may be destroyed. The agency will be prohibited from bringing new inventory into the Central Warehouse.

warehouse_insurance_coverage.description: AGLC insures liquor under all-risk property coverage from acceptance at the Central Warehouse (signed delivery receipt) until loaded for outbound shipment (bill of lading). Settlement basis is the agency's invoice price.

faulty_product_claim_deadline_days.description: Claim for refund on faulty product must be received by AGLC within 30 days of delivery. Customer returns may be refunded up to 1 year from date of invoice.

faulty_product_destruction_requirements.description: Faulty product destruction requires: AGLC written consent, destruction by licensee employee on premises with at least one witness, and video recording retained for minimum 90 days

product_recall_immediate_suspension.description: When AGLC issues a product recall, licensees must immediately suspend sales and remove products from shelves

personal_importation_interprovincial.description: An adult may import liquor purchased in another province for personal use in any quantity if in direct possession or as baggage on the same conveyance. An adult may also cause liquor to be brought in via a Class D retailer or registered agency.
personal_importation_interprovincial.l2laaf_note: Kaskflow could facilitate personal importation requests by connecting consumers with Class D retailers or agencies, but the product must still be registered with AGLC and go through the Central Warehouse

board_to_board_transfers_permitted.description: An agency may initiate transfer of product from Alberta to another provincial liquor jurisdiction at any time by obtaining a purchase order from the receiving jurisdiction. Board to Board transfers are not paid through consignment as the product is not sold in Alberta.

event_team_agreements_eligible_events.description: Event/Team Agreements are permitted only for events open to the general public where the primary purpose is not liquor consumption: community-based entertainment, professional/semi-pro sports, or broad-based cultural events

sponsorship_no_free_liquor.description: Agencies and suppliers may not provide free liquor or exclusively provide liquor products under a sponsorship

contest_no_purchase_required.description: Participation in a contest or raffle must not be conditional on the purchase or consumption of liquor

criminal_charge_reporting_obligation.description: An agency must notify AGLC immediately if they or key employees/directors/shareholders are charged or convicted under: Criminal Code, Excise Act, Food and Drugs Act, Controlled Drugs and Substances Act, Income Tax Act, equivalent foreign acts, GLCA, or GLCR. May result in suspension or cancellation.

liquor_definition_abv_threshold.description: AGLC defines 'liquor' as beverages intended for human consumption containing over 1% alcohol by volume (spirits, wine, liqueur, coolers, cider, beer)
liquor_definition_abv_threshold.l2laaf_note: Products between 0.5% and 1% ABV are not subject to excise duty but ARE subject to AGLC regulation as they exceed the 1% liquor definition — verify against AGLC's interpretation
